prerequisites: A Google Cloud / Firebase project with a Realtime Database instance provisioned.
The Realtime Database name (the instance identifier found in the Firebase console under your project's database settings), used for the required database_name config field.
A service account in the project with access to the Realtime Database, and its generated private key JSON file for the required google_application_credentials field.

authMethods.name: Google service account credentials (JSON key)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate using a Google service account private key JSON, supplied via the google_application_credentials secret field. Firebase exchanges the service account email and private key for an OAuth2 access token covering the https://www.googleapis.com/auth/userinfo.email and https://www.googleapis.com/auth/firebase.database scopes, which is then sent on REST API calls as an Authorization: Bearer token or access_token query parameter.

setupSteps.title: Generate a service account private key
setupSteps.body: In the Firebase console, open Settings > Service accounts. Click Generate New Private Key, then confirm with Generate Key. Securely store the downloaded JSON file. Do not commit it to a public repository or expose it in a client app.

setupSteps.title: Provide the credentials JSON
setupSteps.body: Supply the contents of the downloaded service account JSON file in the required, secret google_application_credentials field. This is the credential the connector uses to obtain an access token for the Realtime Database REST API.

setupSteps.title: Set the database name
setupSteps.body: Enter your Realtime Database instance identifier in the required database_name field. This is the <DATABASE_NAME> component of the database URL https://<DATABASE_NAME>.firebaseio.com, locatable in the Firebase console under your project's database settings.

setupSteps.title: Optionally scope to a path and tune buffering
setupSteps.body: Set the optional path field to read from a specific node in the JSON tree instead of the database root. Set the optional buffer_size field to control how many records are buffered while reading. Leave either unset to use the connector defaults.

sources.title: Add the Firebase Admin SDK to your server (service account key generation)
